title: A small population with strong regional contrasts
content: Norway's population is relatively small and unevenly distributed. Urbanization has grown, but regional identity remains strong, especially where distance and climate shape local life.

title: Aging and migration are central social trends
content: Like many high-income countries, Norway faces population aging and labor-market pressure. Immigration contributes to workforce renewal and social diversity, while integration policy remains politically sensitive.
next: Culture needs to be seen as everyday practice.

title: Traditions and modern life coexist
content: National holidays, outdoor culture, food traditions, sports, and arts all matter, but culture is not static. Global media and migration continuously reshape how identity is expressed.

title: Equality and trust structure social expectations
content: Norms of fairness, low hierarchy, and institutional trust influence schools, workplaces, and political discourse. Work-life balance is widely valued and supported by policy design.
next: Language is part of this social map.

title: Norwegian variation and Sami presence
content: Norwegian includes rich dialect diversity and two written standards, while Sami languages are key to Indigenous rights and regional identity in the north.
next: Social systems convert values into institutions.

title: Education, healthcare, and welfare as shared infrastructure
content: Public systems reduce insecurity across the life course. These institutions are expensive but politically legitimate because most people see them as collective insurance, not narrow benefits.

title: Integration, aging, and center-periphery gaps
content: Key social tensions include integrating new arrivals, sustaining welfare financing with an aging population, and balancing opportunities between major cities and remote regions.
next: The core insight is system interaction.

title: Norwegian society is a high-trust system under adaptation
content: Society in Norway is best understood as institutions plus culture plus demography. The country's strength is coordinated adaptation, not the absence of social pressure.
next: Economy module: how this social model is financed and challenged.
